channel.title: AI Thought Leader Talks (YouTube)
channel.description: Original long-form talks and interviews with AI thought leaders, filtered by Claude.

item.title: [Yann LeCun] "LLMs Are A Dead End": An Exclusive Interview With The Genius Father of AI | Yann LeCun
item.author: This Is The World
item.description:
This Is The World · 51 min

Host Matt Kawecki sits down with Yann LeCun, Turing Award winner and co-founder of AMI Labs, for an exclusive interview. LeCun shares his views on why LLMs are a dead end and what the future of AI research looks like.

item.title: [Yann LeCun] Ep. 3 - A University and Corporate Perspective with Yann LeCun
item.author: Stanford Digital Economy Lab
item.description:
Stanford Digital Economy Lab · 81 min

Tom sits down with Yann LeCun, the Jacob T. Schwartz Professor of Computer Science at NYU and Executive Chairman of Advanced Machine Intelligence Labs. Yann is co-winner of the 2018 ACM Turing Award for his research in neural network learning. He takes us from his days as a postdoc working with Geoff Hinton, through his time as Chief AI Scientist at Facebook/Meta. His simultaneous roles as a Professor at NYU and Chief AI Scientist at a large AI provider give Yann a unique perspective on how technological advances and commercial forces combined to get us to today's state of the art.

item.title: [Yann LeCun] LIVE: Yann LeCun Speaks at India AI Impact Summit
item.author: India Today Global
item.description:
India Today Global · 80 min

AI pioneer Yann LeCun addresses the India AI Impact Summit. Known for his groundbreaking work in deep learning and neural networks, LeCun shares insights on the future of artificial intelligence, machine learning research, open-source AI, and global innovation. As Chief AI Scientist at Meta Platforms, he discusses emerging technologies, responsible AI, large language models, and India's growing role in the global AI ecosystem.

item.title: [Yann LeCun] Yann LeCun: LLMs are limited. AI isn’t dangerous. “World models” are the real bet @ShomaChauduryLL
item.author: Synapse Conclave
item.description:
Synapse Conclave · 41 min

Yann LeCun, Turing Award winner, in conversation with Shoma Chaudhury at Synapse Conclave. He unpacks the hype around LLMs, explains why there is disagreement on how to get to superintelligence, discusses whether AI is dangerous, why he left Meta, and his vision for making machines smarter than humans through "world models."

item.title: [Yann LeCun] AI Research Symposium: The Next Frontiers | Keynotes by Demis Hassabis, Yoshua Bengio & Yann LeCun
item.author: IndiaAI
item.description:
IndiaAI · 467 min

The Research Symposium on Enabling AI at Nation Scale, hosted by the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY). This landmark event features keynote addresses from Turing Award winners Demis Hassabis, Yoshua Bengio, and Yann LeCun, exploring how AI acts as a catalyst for scientific breakthroughs and the grand challenges facing the global research community.

item.title: [Yann LeCun] "Perspectives on IA" : conf. de Yann LeCun, WinterWeek – Graduate School – Univ. Gustave Eiffel
item.author: Université Gustave Eiffel
item.description:
Université Gustave Eiffel · 126 min

Yann LeCun, 2018 Turing Award winner for his work on deep learning, delivers a lecture titled "Perspectives on IA" at the WinterWeek for Research Training of the Graduate School at Université Gustave Eiffel on February 11, 2026. LeCun is a professor at NYU, former Chief AI Scientist at Meta, and founder of AMI Labs. He presents his project to revive research on "predictive" AI and the development of a new approach based on "world models."

item.title: [Geoffrey Hinton] The AI Scenario Nobody Wants To Talk About | Geoffrey Hinton (The Diary Of A CEO)
item.author: The Lumen Project
item.description:
The Lumen Project · 27 min

Geoffrey Hinton, often called the "Godfather of AI," discusses the risks of artificial intelligence in this conversation from The Diary of a CEO. After over 50 years helping build the neural network technology powering today's AI revolution, Hinton explains why the technology he spent his life developing could become one of humanity's greatest risks. Topics include autonomous weapons, AI-driven cyber attacks, the possibility of superintelligent machines, and whether humans will remain in control. Chapters: 00:03 - The 'Godfather of AI' and Neural Networks' Early Days 01:40 - The Dawn of AI Risks and Hinton's Warning Mission 03:00 - Understanding AI's Leap: Digital vs. Biological Learning 04:57 - Distinguishing AI Threats: Misuse vs. Existential Risk 07:09 - Why AI Development Cannot Be Stopped 09:55 - AI-Powered Cyber Attacks and Personal Security 13:46 - The Threat of AI-Designed Biological Viruses 15:19 - AI's Role in Corrupting Elections and Creating Echo Chambers 19:21 - The Global Challenge of Regulating AI 23:48 - Lethal Autonomous Weapons and Catastrophic Scenarios

item.title: [Jensen Huang] Morgan Stanley TMT Conference 2026 | Jensen Huang on AI, Compute, Tokens and the New Global Economy
item.author: Morgan Stanley
item.description:
Morgan Stanley · 43 min

From the Morgan Stanley Technology, Media & Telecom Conference in San Francisco, NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang explains how AI is reshaping the global economy. His core insight: compute creates tokens, tokens power intelligence, and intelligence translates into economic output — at every level, from companies to countries.

item.title: [Jensen Huang] Jensen Huang: Nvidia's Future, Physical AI, Rise of the Agent, Inference Explosion, AI PR Crisis
item.author: All-In Podcast
item.description:
All-In Podcast · 66 min

Jensen Huang joins the All-In Podcast to discuss Nvidia's future. Topics include: acquiring Groq and the inference explosion, decision making at the world's most valuable company, Physical AI's $50T market, OpenClaw's future, AI's PR crisis, refuting doomer narratives, revenue capacity, agentic future, open source and global diffusion, self-driving platform, datacenters in space, AI healthcare, robotics, and advice to young people on excelling in the AI era. Chapters: 0:00 Jensen Huang joins the show 1:00 Acquiring Groq and the inference explosion 9:27 Decision making at the world's most valuable company 11:22 Physical AI's $50T market, OpenClaw's future 17:12 AI's PR crisis, refuting doomer narratives 21:22 Revenue capacity, agentic future 31:24 Open source, global diffusion 40:19 Self-driving platform, competition 48:06 Datacenters in space, AI healthcare, robotics 56:44 OpenAI/Anthropic revenue potential 59:38 Advice to young people

item.title: [Jensen Huang] NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang GTC 2026 Full Keynote
item.author: Yahoo Finance
item.description:
Yahoo Finance · 140 min

Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang delivers his annual GTC 2026 keynote speech in San Jose, California. The event comes amid Nvidia's recent dealmaking spree, including its acqui-hire agreement with AI inference chip designer Groq. Also covered: Nvidia's strategic partner Nebius reaching a deal with Meta to provide up to $27 billion worth of capacity starting in 2027.

item.title: [Org: OpenAI Talks] Building AI for better healthcare — the OpenAI Podcast Ep. 14
item.author: OpenAI
item.description:
OpenAI · 31 min

OpenAI's Head of Health Dr. Nate Gross and Karan Singhal, who leads Health AI Research, discuss how AI can help address the biggest challenges in healthcare. They cover how OpenAI is training models to handle sensitive health questions in collaboration with physicians, and how that foundation is unlocking a new generation of tools for patients, clinicians, and healthcare systems. Chapters: 00:00:38 Origins of Nate and Karan's interest in AI and healthcare 00:05:01 Strategy for building AI tools for clinicians 00:06:57 How AI models are trained for health use cases 00:10:15 How OpenAI is able to score well on health evals 00:14:21 Key challenges deploying AI in healthcare 00:21:05 Collaboration with hospitals and healthcare systems 00:23:05 Practical everyday uses of AI health assistants 00:26:43 Biggest "wow" moment during development 00:28:46 Feedback from clinicians and early users

item.title: [Org: Meta AI Talks] Meta NeuroScientist SHOCKED Me: Scale Alone Won’t Create Consciousness!
item.author: Dr Brian Keating
item.description:
Dr Brian Keating · 51 min

Dr. David Sussillo (Meta Reality Labs, Stanford) explains why scaling transformers alone won't produce conscious AI and why studying the brain may be the only way forward. Topics include: why scaling alone won't produce consciousness, how recurrent neural networks differ from transformers, the FORCE algorithm for training RNNs, Meta's EMG wristband project, Rich Sutton's Bitter Lesson, and why studying the brain may be the only way out of AI's current limitations. Chapters: 00:00 Scale alone won't produce consciousness 00:25 The bitter lesson: big data crushes every algorithmic invention 01:15 How hallucinations were discovered early at Google 05:00 The lock-in problem: why LLM + GPU may cap AI's ceiling 08:25 What Meta's neural wristband is actually trying to solve 11:20 Rich Sutton's bitter lesson explained 14:15 "More is Different" — why you can't reduce complexity upward 28:30 The book Emergence — two stories braided into one 36:00 Why mentorship matters more than intelligence for at-risk kids 47:00 Stability over intensity: what kids in foster care actually need

item.title: [Channel: Y Combinator] How AI Is Unlocking Millions Of New Builders
item.author: Y Combinator
item.description:
Y Combinator · 40 min

On The Lightcone, Mukund and Madhav Jha, founders of Emergent, discuss their AI platform that lets anyone build and ship production-ready software. In just eight months, users have created more than 7 million apps on Emergent, with the number doubling in the last 45 days. They discuss how they built one of the most powerful AI coding agents, why they focused on non-technical users, and what it's like building for a global audience from India. Chapters: 00:00 Intro 01:06 What Is Emergent? 02:09 From AI Testing to General Coding Agents 04:18 The Pivot to Non-Technical Users 09:04 Building for Production, Not Just Prototypes 18:21 Live Demo: Building Apps with Emergent 24:40 How Emergent Hires and Runs a Lean Team 29:04 Is SaaS Dead? The Rise of Personalized Software 34:04 The Future: Niche Apps, Solo Builders and AI Agency

item.title: [Channel: Zhang Xiaojun Podcast] A 7-hour marathon interview with Saining Xie: World Models, AMI Labs, Yann LeCun, Fei-Fei Li, and 42
item.author: Zhang Xiaojun Podcast
item.description:
Zhang Xiaojun Podcast · 405 min

A 7-hour marathon interview with Saining Xie, co-founder and Chief Science Officer of AMI Labs. Xie, alongside Turing Award laureate Yann LeCun, launched AMI Labs to pursue "World Models" research. The company secured a $1.03 billion Seed round at a $3.5 billion pre-money valuation with just 25 people and zero products — the largest Seed round in European history. Born in 1990, Xie is an alumnus of Shanghai Jiao Tong University and UC San Diego, and holds a faculty position at NYU. He was previously a Research Scientist at Google DeepMind and spent four years at Meta's FAIR. He is best known for co-authoring Diffusion Transformers (DiT). This is Xie's first-ever in-depth interview. Topics span 15 years of AI technological revolutions: why LLMs are not a success story for the Bitter Lesson, why language model Scaling Laws have "water" in them, why language is "poison" for vision research, his friendship with Kaiming He, turning down Ilya Sutskever twice, memories of Yann LeCun and Fei-Fei Li, what world models are, the genesis of AMI Labs, and why "Silicon Valley is hypnotized" by LLMs. Chapters: 00:01:19 The Normal One 00:35:40 The World Won't Let Me Do Vision 00:52:06 Academic Nomadism 00:57:43 A Friendship with Kaiming He 01:05:35 Turning Down Ilya Twice 01:08:26 Memories of Yann LeCun and Fei-Fei Li 01:12:18 Hidden Clues: A World of Representations 02:43:55 Research Taste and the Diamond Sutra 04:11:07 What is a World Model? 04:29:47 From Downloading the Internet to Downloading Humanity 04:58:17 The Genesis of AMI Labs with Yann LeCun 05:45:53 "Silicon Valley is Hypnotized" 06:07:17 The Arrogance of Mankind 06:18:45 "42"
